title: Salty Velvety Saudia Tamarind Bowl
description: A savory and velvety brunch-mood Saudi Arabian dish featuring tamarind balls as a base with a touch of sweetness and saltiness. This delightful bowl is completely plant-based-friendly, perfect for those looking to enjoy a flavorful meal.

ingredients.name: Plant-Based Tofu
ingredients.notes: Select extra firm tofu for better texture.

ingredients.name: Sundried Tomato Puree
ingredients.notes: Optional, add if desired for a richer flavor profile.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with vegan butter or non-dairy margarine.

ingredients.name: Garam Masala Powder
ingredients.notes: To taste, can be adjusted based on spice preference.

ingredients.name: Cumin Powder
ingredients.notes: Used for added depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Coriander Powder
ingredients.notes: Can enhance the earthy and herbaceous notes in the dish.

ingredients.name: Water or Plant-Based Stock
ingredients.notes: Use filtered water for best results, as well as a plant-based stock to avoid artificial additives.

ingredients.name: Cumin Seeds
ingredients.notes: For added aroma and depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Saffron Threads
ingredients.notes: Use for a slight hint of color and flavor, optional.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust based on personal preference for saltiness level.

ingredients.name: Fresh Tamarind Paste
ingredients.notes: Use if fresh tamarind is available, otherwise use a jarred paste to save time and effort.

ingredients.name: Green Chilies (optional)
ingredients.notes: Add for heat; adjust quantity as per your taste preference.

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the tofu with sundried tomato puree and saffron threads if using.
Drizzle olive oil over the mixture and sprinkle garam masala, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Mix well to ensure even coating.
Heat 2 teaspoons of olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Place the tofu mixture in the pan and cook for about 5 minutes on each side until golden brown.
In another small bowl, mix water or plant-based stock with salt. Stir gently to dissolve the salt completely and let it cool slightly.

Heat up some water or plant-based stock (about half a cup) in a medium saucepan. Once hot, add fresh tamarind paste to the heated liquid and simmer for about 5 minutes until reduced and thickened. Add sundried tomato puree if using and stir gently until fully combined.
Pour the deglazed tofu mixture into the pan with the tamarind liquid. Continue cooking over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has absorbed by half, creating a velvety texture.
Once done, remove from heat and let it cool for about 5 minutes before serving in individual bowls.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or parsley leaves, green chilies (if used), and additional sundried tomatoes if desired. Serve hot as a breakfast side dish.
